metadata.title: Claude, Allowed.

metadata.greeting_note: HAI persona, shortest-form rule. 'Hi' = one word.
metadata.thesis: The ban is a rule about homework. It is not a prophecy about you.
metadata.note: ILLUSTRATE LAW reel: UI at B00 (cold open with RESULT lines), B06 (verdict artifact), B07 (handoff), B08 (outro). beats.narration_text: Your school banned AI — but not your future. Three things: what the ban actually covers, what it doesn't, and the one rule that keeps you clean either way. Let's read the policy, not the headline.

props.command: my school banned AI — now what?
output: bans cover: graded work submitted without disclosure
bans don't cover: fluency practice on your own time
the safe rule: ask your teacher — get it in writing

beats.narration_text: Most school AI bans say roughly the same thing: don't use AI to do work you're supposed to do yourself and submit it without saying so. That's the scope — graded work, undisclosed. Not: don't ever use AI. Not: don't get fluent. Not: don't practice on your own time. Read the sentence, not the story around it.

openItems: fluency practice on your own time

beats.narration_text: Here's where it gets slippery. Nearly every policy has an exception — something like 'except for educational purposes.' Teachers interpret this differently. One thinks it means any classroom use with permission. Another thinks it means nothing AI, full stop. You don't want to find out which one your teacher is after you've already submitted. The rule is: ask before you use, and get the answer in writing. Even an email reply counts. Assumptions here cost grade points.

props.safeMove: ask your teacher · get it in writing

beats.narration_text: What's yours, regardless of any policy: fluency. You can use Claude on your own time to practice prompting, to quiz yourself on your own material, to get explanations for things you're confused about — as long as you're not submitting AI output as your own work. Anthropic's AI Fluency course is Creative Commons and free. A ban on AI in graded homework is not a ban on becoming capable.

openItems: practicing prompting on your own time
quizzing yourself on your own material

beats.narration_text: Before the verdict — one question to commit to: what actually gets students caught? The use itself — or something else? Think before the next beat flips.

props.question: What actually gets students caught — using AI, or something else?
props.commit: commit to an answer before the next beat

beats.narration_text: The answer: it's not the use. It's the undisclosed use. Students caught for AI violations aren't punished for being AI-fluent. They're punished for submitting AI-assisted work without saying so. Same output — different path. Disclosure doesn't make the work automatically fine, but it puts you in the conversation instead of outside it.

So the policy collapses to one question: did you tell your teacher? That's it. A rule about disclosure, applied to graded work. The working world you're walking into runs on AI. The ban is about this semester's homework. Getting fluent — legally, on your own time, with disclosure — is not cheating. It's preparation.

artifactLines: Bans cover: graded work submitted without disclosure — not fluency, not practice on your own time.
The exemption trap: 'educational purposes' means different things to different teachers — ask first, get it in writing.
What's yours: AI Fluency course (CC, free), home practice, ungraded skill-building — a ban doesn't ban becoming capable.
The one rule: disclose. A disclosed use is a conversation. An undisclosed one is a violation.

beats.narration_text: Your move: don't assume — ask. Paste this into Claude with your actual assignment. You'll get a short honest email to send your teacher. The reply tells you what's actually allowed in your context, and puts you on the right side of the policy before you start.

props.command: Draft me a short, honest email to my teacher asking whether I can use Claude for [describe your specific assignment or subject]. I want to start the right conversation — keep it one paragraph.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Allowed. If this cleared the fog, subscribe. HAI.
